119307.explanation: For us, the quality of the people in the council is much more important than gender.

119308.explanation: After the elections, this is the most important means of participation.

119310.explanation: Basically, we embrace such initiatives. However, we believe they must fit into the environment.

119311.explanation: Distribution centers, like in Den Hoorn (Harnaschpolder), do not contribute substantially to employment.

119312.explanation: According to us, the large-scale asylum seeker center for 225 asylum seekers has too great an impact on the residents of Den Hoorn in this case. We do favor small-scale shelter for families distributed across the villages.

119313.explanation: Housing for employees is an integral part of business operations. Unfortunately, seasonal workers currently compete with our residents on the overheated housing market.

119317.explanation: Given the upcoming fireworks ban, we believe the municipality should offer an alternative.

119318.explanation: Given the increasing number of illegal waste dumps, we believe that a price incentive has a counterproductive effect, resulting in environmental pollution.

119320.explanation: The local broadcaster is the voice of the municipality.

119321.explanation: We believe that a financially healthy association contributes to the health/vitality of residents.

119323.explanation: In the short term, we embrace this statement. However, we believe more police presence is needed on the streets of Midden-Delfland for the long term.

119325.explanation: People eligible for placement in a domestic violence shelter must be prioritized when allocating housing, anonymously of course.

119327.explanation: We believe that residents of our municipality should be prioritized in the allocation of social rental housing.

119331.explanation: We believe every resident of Midden-Delfland receiving social assistance benefits should be given the opportunity to participate again in society by performing a socially beneficial task. Everyone participates.

119332.explanation: It is the municipality's task to financially support residents with a low income below the subsistence level. At the same time, as a municipality, we must ensure these people re-engage in society so they become less dependent on the municipality.

119335.explanation: The quality of care is paramount.

119339.explanation: Looking at small entrepreneurs, such as B&B owners, we see that this creates too much administrative burden for them. In addition, a tourist tax is uninviting for tourists.

119342.explanation: We are against discrimination based on origin. Equal monks, equal hoods.

119349.explanation: We disagree with this because the demand for social rental housing from residents of Midden-Delfland is significantly lower than 30%. The reason that a minimum of 30% is established comes from Woonnet Haaglanden.

119351.explanation: The most important criterion is that the parking balance is not compromised.

119352.explanation: In Midden-Delfland, 41% of the residents feel lonely. To address this loneliness, we believe that a village center can play an important role in reducing this percentage.

119353.explanation: In Den Hoorn, we want to bring back the village square. At the moment, it is just an upgraded parking lot.

119355.explanation: If we look at what the municipality has added so far in terms of recreational functions in the rural area, it is disappointing. We can add more here. A simple example: make the bike paths in the rural area wider so they become more accessible for cycling side by side.

119356.explanation: This is part of our housing mix. We need to look at what is possible, not what is impossible.

119357.explanation: We believe that electric trucks are not the future. We are more oriented towards hybrid vehicles.

119359.explanation: From an environmental perspective, we find this unnecessary and undesirable. A mailbox without a sticker is automatically yes-yes.

119307.explanation: The CDA strives for equal representation between men and women. We should not overdo it, because ultimately individual qualities are decisive.

119308.explanation: In the Netherlands, we have chosen a system of representation. Citizens elect council members, and council members vote on decisions. This is a good system because it allows council members to develop expertise on the dossiers they have to vote on on behalf of the citizens.

119310.explanation: We encourage farmers to diversify. This way, small and medium-sized farms can continue to exist that fit the landscape and character of Midden-Delfland.

119311.explanation: Where space is available, this need not be a problem. However, in our municipality, there is little room for new distribution centers. The CDA finds it important to steer this carefully so that the right company ends up in the right location.

119312.explanation: Closing the AZC in the short term is not possible because the municipality has contractually committed itself for several years. It makes no sense to make statements during election time that are legally unattainable. Moreover, financial coverage is arranged for the agreements made.

119313.explanation: The question does not state that it concerns foreign seasonal workers/labor migrants. The CDA welcomes that employers take their role in addressing the housing problem. This cannot be asserted generally.

119315.explanation: The CDA believes that especially the use of charcoal causes nuisance when it is not done properly or at the wrong time. We advocate clear communication and good information so that residents know how to handle this responsibly.

119317.explanation: The CDA is positive about a possible citizen initiative to organize this.

119318.explanation: Collecting bulky waste without a contribution costs everyone money. The CDA wants to encourage residents to go to the recycling center themselves. This promotes waste separation and reduces costs for the municipality.

119321.explanation: The CDA supports sports clubs warmly because this contributes to community feeling. There are already subsidy agreements between the municipality and sports clubs for major investments like buildings and sports fields.

119323.explanation: If there is a case of insecurity, it can be considered whether a greater deployment of enforcement officers (BOAs) is desirable. At this moment, this is not the case.

119325.explanation: Midden-Delfland cooperates with surrounding municipalities and has made agreements. We cannot demand that a shelter for domestic violence victims be located in our municipality because these locations remain secret for safety reasons.

119327.explanation: The CDA is fully committed to realizing this within legal possibilities. A maximum of 50% can be allocated for local customization. It cannot be applied to all homes.

119331.explanation: The CDA supports meaningful social work for people receiving social assistance benefits. This increases participation and self-worth. Attention must be paid to exceptions, such as single parents with young children.

119332.explanation: The CDA wants to maintain debt forgiveness for people with low incomes. Stopping it would increase poverty and debts. This arrangement provides security of existence and prevents social costs. Solidarity and equal opportunities are central to us.

119335.explanation: It will unfortunately never be possible always. The municipality is responsible for a diverse range of care providers.

119339.explanation: Compared to other municipalities, the tourist tax in Midden-Delfland is already low.

119342.explanation: The municipality has the duty to comply with the established task.

119345.explanation: The Dorpshoeve was renovated not so long ago, and the facilities are suitable and meet the demand.

119347.explanation: The CDA recognizes the importance of swimming diplomas but does not see the necessity for mandatory school swimming. For families who cannot afford swimming lessons, the municipality provides financial support so every child can swim safely.

119349.explanation: These are agreements included in the housing vision. The CDA supports these agreements.

119351.explanation: The CDA wants to explore the extent to which it is possible to introduce, for example, landlady rental or 'friends contracts' to make better use of homes. This must fit the housing and meet various requirements.

119353.explanation: There are sufficient parking spaces in the villages of Midden-Delfland.

119356.explanation: In Midden-Delfland there is a location where various tiny houses have been placed. The CDA is positive about this, and it meets a certain need. Given the limited space within the municipality, it does not seem realistic to designate multiple locations.

119357.explanation: Electric garbage trucks (side loaders) are already operating in Midden-Delfland.

119358.explanation: The CDA wants to make sure that vacant buildings are occupied as much as possible. We realize that this requires time and effort from the municipality. Furthermore, the question is whether the problem is big enough and whether this measure has a truly significant effect.

119359.explanation: The CDA supports the introduction of the yes-yes sticker. Everyone remains free to choose.

119307.explanation: OGP finds diversity in the council important but also that the right person is in the right place. Additionally, the council should reflect society. So, we prioritize quality.

119308.explanation: OGP stands for resident meetings in which everyone can participate in discussions about important topics.

119310.explanation: OGP advocates that farmers combine nature management with their business operations. Additionally, a farm can start a shop and/or camping. However, this is not always possible (e.g., a suitable infrastructure must be in place).

119311.explanation: With the business park 'Harnaschpolder' in Den Hoorn, the municipality has done enough regarding distribution centers. OGP wants Midden-Delfland to remain the green lungs of an urban area. So, no new space should be sacrificed for new distribution centers.

119312.explanation: As OGP, we stand for keeping our agreements: this means that we make asylum reception possible. We also stand for solidarity in practice: we will ensure good reception for refugees and newcomers.

119313.explanation: Employers are obliged under the law to ensure good landlordship for adequate housing, and in consultation with the municipality, what is possible regarding housing for labor migrants can be explored.

119315.explanation: As OGP, we stand and move forward for clean air. That means we want to restrict the use of fireplaces and wood stoves in densely built village centers.

119317.explanation: As OGP, we support a fireworks ban. We prefer digital techniques, but as a municipality, we do want to facilitate fireworks shows.

119318.explanation: The polluter pays is OGP's motto. This means that a small contribution may be requested when collecting bulky waste.

119320.explanation: It is important that there is also local level reporting. That means the municipality must contribute to the local broadcaster.

119321.explanation: OGP supports that everyone within Midden-Delfland can participate in sports. That means we contribute to good fields and such. If a club struggles due to unforeseen circumstances, the municipality can provide assistance. In other cases, no.

119323.explanation: Currently, the municipality of Midden-Delfland has two municipal enforcement officers. That seems sufficient, as long as it is clear what is expected of these people.

119325.explanation: As OGP, we find it important that people (especially women) can live safely. That means there must be a place where people can go if they feel unsafe.

119327.explanation: As OGP, we believe that the agreement that a maximum of 50% of homes is allocated to local residents should be fully implemented. However, we are also part of the Haaglanden region, so residents of Midden-Delfland can also obtain housing in the rest of the region.

119331.explanation: Not everyone is able to contribute. Therefore, for OGP, where possible, people can do socially meaningful work.

119332.explanation: For OGP, no one should be left behind. Midden-Delfland is a municipality where we look out for each other. That means we take care of people with low incomes.

119335.explanation: OGP finds 'always' too absolute a starting point. If it is possible because the municipality has contracted it, that is of course great, but it is not a goal in itself.

119339.explanation: Our tourist tax is modest and in line with other comparable municipalities. We think that is fine: tourists using facilities within Midden-Delfland should contribute as well.

119342.explanation: This is currently regulated by law and the municipality must comply with the numbers imposed by the national government. If that changes, we believe a good balance must be found for rental housing for refugees and housing seekers that fits the number of rental homes in the municipality.

119345.explanation: OGP finds it important that the Dorpshoeve remains a place for meeting and a center for various activities in the village. Therefore, renewal is necessary somewhere in the coming years.

119347.explanation: As far as OGP is concerned, that is unnecessary because it is too general. Where parents cannot afford swimming lessons for their children, we find that the municipality can provide financial help.

119349.explanation: OGP finds it important that homes are also available for people who cannot buy a house or rent a more expensive one. In 2022, we ensured through an adopted motion that 30% social housing in new construction is now policy in the municipality.

119351.explanation: If this can be done in a way where people can live properly, this offers opportunities according to OGP. If it means people are 'locked up' with negative consequences for themselves and the environment, we do not see this as a good solution.

119352.explanation: According to OGP, much work is already underway; current plans focus on lively village centers within the villages. Think, for example, of the redesign of Schipluiden and the new center of Den Hoorn.

119353.explanation: As far as OGP is concerned, the current policy suffices. We prefer to invest in better, more sustainable facilities such as shared cars, space for bicycle parking, and greening existing parking lots.

119355.explanation: The strength of preserving Midden-Delfland is its unique countryside. The more people know and visit it, the better we can protect it. OGP finds it logical that the municipality invests in tourism on a scale suitable for the area.

119356.explanation: OGP is the initiator of the first pilot for Tiny Houses on Woudselaan in Den Hoorn and certainly sees this housing form as an added value that fits the values of Midden-Delfland and for those who want it.

119357.explanation: As far as OGP is concerned, only if this is done together with other municipalities that use the same garbage trucks. Otherwise, it is too expensive an investment for just our municipality.

119358.explanation: As OGP, we support encouraging and supporting the continued habitation of buildings. We find a mandatory requirement too far-reaching.

119359.explanation: OGP considers the current provision fine: people who want no mail or only certain mail can indicate this with a sticker.

119307.explanation: Quality comes first. Whether someone is male or female is irrelevant. However, it is good to have a team that complements each other. Different traits and skills should be present.

119308.explanation: Democracy benefits most from a referendum, which should not be an advisory referendum.

119310.explanation: The countryside of Midden-Delfland must retain an open and green character. Allowing every farmer to open a campsite leads to clutter. Every situation is unique and must be assessed as such.

119311.explanation: When the zoning plan provides for the establishment of new distribution centers or any activity, the municipality must refrain from measures that prevent this. No new locations should be designated for distribution centers.

119312.explanation: The large-scale asylum seeker center in Den Hoorn does not fit the village character. The consequences of this center, including the pressure on facilities and social cohesion in Den Hoorn, have not been well considered.

119313.explanation: That depends on the number of seasonal workers needed and the availability of other suitable housing.

119315.explanation: Residents should be restricted in their rights by the municipality as little as possible.

119317.explanation: Municipalities should focus as much as possible on their core tasks, and organizing fireworks shows is not one of those. Municipalities should allow entrepreneurs to take such initiatives.

119318.explanation: Residents can drop off their bulky waste for free at the currently designated points. Pickup costs EUR 35, which is below the cost price of the service.

119320.explanation: Government bodies and institutions financed with public funds must use available resources efficiently and frugally. If WOS cannot make ends meet financially and demonstrates that cuts are impossible, the municipality can potentially step in.

119321.explanation: The community life is important and should be supported if necessary to survive. However, clubs must also follow prudent financial policies, with the principle that they should support themselves.

119323.explanation: Every resident and visitor of Midden-Delfland must feel safe, and when necessary, deploying more municipal enforcement officers is an appropriate instrument.

119325.explanation: No need for this has been demonstrated. Midden-Delfland should focus on keeping big city problems out.

119327.explanation: The VVD has this as a spearhead in their program.

119331.explanation: Every citizen should contribute to society according to their ability.

119332.explanation: Everyone contributes proportionally to the costs incurred by the municipality.

119335.explanation: Provided it is cost-neutral or at one's own expense, it is the individual's choice, which the government does not control. When the government pays, there is no choice, and neutral care is offered.

119339.explanation: The municipality must be attractive to tourists and not drive them away with high taxes. However, tourism is a source of income that can fund expenses promoting tourism.

119342.explanation: Status holders should not have priority positions.

119345.explanation: There must be redevelopment at that location including space for housing while preserving certain facilities currently organized there.

119347.explanation: Swimming lessons are the responsibility of parents.

119349.explanation: Research has shown that Midden-Delfland has no need for more social rental homes. These are built only for the Haaglanden region and allocated through Woonnet Haaglanden. Instead, more expensive and mid-priced homes should be realized for local residents.

119351.explanation: It depends. If there is sufficient parking on private premises, the VVD sees no objection. Otherwise, it depends on whether there is enough free space in the area.

119352.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities in other (association) buildings for activities currently provided in the village centers.

119353.explanation: However, the existing number of parking spaces must be maintained.

119355.explanation: The municipality can make a limited contribution available. It is the entrepreneurs' responsibility, not the municipality's task.

119356.explanation: The municipality must focus on building sustainable, future-proof homes in appropriate locations.

119357.explanation: Whether electric garbage trucks will be introduced depends on the technical and economic lifespan of the current trucks and what is most economically feasible when new trucks need to be ordered.

119358.explanation: No property owner leaves a building vacant without a valid reason. The municipality should not interfere but work constructively to ensure permits are quickly granted within the zoning plan, minimizing vacancy.

119359.explanation: The 'no-no' sticker prevents mailboxes from being polluted with unsolicited advertising material. The government should not interfere further with unnecessary rules that restrict entrepreneurs' freedom to do business.

119307.explanation: We find it important that the executive board reflects society well, including gender balance. At the same time, we do not favor a strict 50/50 requirement. Quality, motivation, and cooperation must lead. Of course, we actively encourage diversity.

119308.explanation: We believe it is important for residents to be involved in major decisions. A referendum often limits to a simple yes or no. Other forms of participation allow residents to also provide ideas, concerns, and suggestions, which often yields more.

119310.explanation: D66 wants farmers to have room for side activities such as a shop or campsite. This supports their income, promotes regional products, and connects farmers and citizens, while considering scale and environment.

119311.explanation: The employment provided by distribution centers is important. That does not mean every center fits our municipality. We focus on sustainable businesses and innovative entrepreneurship that align with Midden-Delfland’s landscape and scale.

119312.explanation: Due to the dispersal law and our moral duty, we accommodate war refugees. Paying to avoid this is expensive and solves nothing. We have labor shortages and therefore focus on language, education, and work. Good integration contributes to understanding and connection in society.

119313.explanation: Housing should not only depend on employment. Losing a job often means losing housing. D66 wants seasonal workers to have decent and independent housing with help from the municipality and employers.

119315.explanation: Clean air is important, especially for people with lung conditions. Therefore, we follow the stove guide. On some days, burning is unwise. We do not support a total ban as long as usage causes no nuisance.

119317.explanation: New Year's Eve should be a connecting celebration. A central fireworks show can contribute to this. The municipality does not have to organize it itself but can facilitate or encourage it together with residents or associations.

119318.explanation: Those who want bulky waste collected pay a contribution themselves. This keeps costs fairly distributed and encourages conscious waste management.

119320.explanation: Local journalism is indispensable for engagement and reliable information. A strong broadcaster deserves support, including financially, provided there is support, quality, and relevance for residents.

119321.explanation: Sports clubs contribute to health and social encounters. If a club with great social value temporarily faces difficulties, support can be appropriate, but with clear criteria to avoid automatic assistance.

119323.explanation: Community officers contribute to safety and livability, such as near schools or dealing with traffic nuisance. More visibility in evenings and weekends is valuable if aimed at approachability and connection.

119325.explanation: Everyone must feel safe. Domestic violence also occurs in Midden-Delfland. Therefore, we support victim shelters in cooperation with the region. Safety and proper assistance are essential, especially for women and children.

119327.explanation: Residents connected to our villages must have a chance at a social rental home. Current rules carefully provide for this. We see no reason to change this policy.

119331.explanation: Everyone participates in their own way. For some, volunteering fits; others may temporarily do nothing. We continuously look for possibilities, not coercion.

119332.explanation: Waivers occur rarely. People who truly cannot pay are not helped by fines or coercion. Without help, costs for the municipality actually increase. We prefer to help people find work or education so they can participate and pay again.

119335.explanation: We find it important that care matches a person's background. At the same time, good care must come first. A choice is not always possible, as long as care providers work respectfully and culturally aware.

119339.explanation: Tourist tax helps fund amenities like bike paths and benches. Visitors benefit from these, so a fair contribution is logical. The tourist tax in Midden-Delfland is much lower than in surrounding municipalities. We do not want our residents to bear all costs.

119342.explanation: Everyone deserves a fair chance at housing. Status holders and other home seekers are distributed fairly. Building more creates space for both new and existing residents.

119345.explanation: The Dorpshoeve is important for Schipluiden. It must be future-proof: more energy-efficient, multifunctional, and suitable for current needs, without unnecessary new construction.

119347.explanation: Swimming lessons are important, and our children have good offerings through existing clubs. We see no need to introduce municipal school swimming now.

119349.explanation: Only 21% of housing in Midden-Delfland is social rental, while people working here in healthcare, childcare, education, or supermarkets also want to live or remain living in our villages.

119351.explanation: Room rentals can help make better use of existing living space, especially now many houses are underoccupied. It must be done properly and responsibly, with attention to neighborhood livability.

119352.explanation: Every village in Midden-Delfland has a village center, and we want to keep it that way. A place where people meet, gather for groceries, a chat, or a cup of coffee. That is the heartbeat of each village, and we cherish it.

119353.explanation: We want our village centers to remain livable and attractive. That means space for greenery, cycling, and social interaction. More parking spaces are not always the solution. However, we do consider each location to find a good balance, without making facilities inaccessible.

119355.explanation: Tourism in the rural area is already well supported through Puuur, funded by entrepreneurs and tourist tax. The municipality does not need to add extra money as long as it continues to run well in this way.

119356.explanation: Tiny houses are not the solution to all housing questions, but can contribute to affordable living with a small ecological footprint in suitable locations, for example on the edges of villages.

119357.explanation: Electric garbage trucks cause less emissions in our villages. That is better for the environment and for the people who collect our waste daily. Fortunately, they are already being used more and more.

119358.explanation: We want to counteract vacancy, especially in village centers. Compelling owners is not always possible, but we do want to engage with them more actively and encourage (temporary) use.

119359.explanation: Less paper waste is important. The yes-yes sticker helps with that, but local newspapers and information must continue to be delivered. Clear agreements need to be made about this.

119307.explanation: We are not dogmatic about this. It is nice/recommended for the council to include at least one woman and one man.

119308.explanation: National experiences with referenda teach us that these tend to disrupt rather than strengthen the regular democratic process and its quality.

119310.explanation: This requires a tailored approach. Given the current situation of farming and the future of farmers, we support possible alternative income models for farmers, but the impact on the rural area must not be underestimated.

119311.explanation: On the one hand, consumers demand this due to the Internet economy; on the other hand, most of Midden Delfland is not really suitable for it. But there are suitable locations in the Harnaschpolder.

119312.explanation: This goes against the current policy line; as a municipality, we have chosen a medium-term commitment towards the asylum seekers and status holders housed here. We advocate for a reliable government.

119313.explanation: The most important thing is that seasonal workers are not immediately left on the street if they cannot work for any reason. Flexibility is needed but also some commitment from the employer.

119315.explanation: National policy is needed for this. If it is implemented, we will comply.

119317.explanation: This could be a good idea for the coming year, but it must be carefully examined and considered from all sides, including the financial consequences.

119318.explanation: Collecting at home is a good idea in principle, but there should be some compensation per trip.

119320.explanation: In principle, no. Additional support may only be considered if the local broadcaster faces unexpected financial setbacks and cannot properly or at all fulfill its mission.

119321.explanation: The most important thing is a fair and just policy where one association is not disproportionately supported compared to another. Too much support leads to artificial preservation of associations with mismanagement.

119323.explanation: The current situation is acceptable; there is no reason to change it.

119325.explanation: On the one hand, safe shelter is desirable and current capacity is tight; on the other hand, financial resources are limited. The question is where those resources can be used most effectively.

119327.explanation: Connection with the local community is indeed desirable, although legally not feasible (and perhaps not entirely desirable) for all available homes. We generally aim for 50% allocation with preference to local residents.

119331.explanation: This is not flexible and ultimately even disrespectful: part of the target group would very much like to contribute socially but simply cannot.

119332.explanation: We support the current exemption-from-payment policy.

119335.explanation: In principle, yes; a good match between caregiver and care recipient increases the experienced quality of and satisfaction with care.

119339.explanation: This is fine as it is. It should be evaluated halfway through the year.

119342.explanation: We support the previous national policy with limited allocation of homes to refugees with residency permits. Abolishing this policy condemns these people to prolonged stays in asylum seeker centers, further clogging the asylum chain.

119345.explanation: A substantial budget has already been allocated to renew the entire center of Schipluiden. When the Dorpshoeve is next, it must be looked at integrally.

119349.explanation: We do not trust that the market will simply build the right homes. Multi-generational homes and senior hofjes, both for purchase and rent, are needed.

119351.explanation: The municipality should be cautious in banning rental forms to (among others) young adults; otherwise, they cannot leave their parents’ homes. Also, the increase in divorces and housing market tightness call for a flexible rental policy.

119352.explanation: Strengthening the social fabric is urgently needed; village communities are under pressure.

119353.explanation: There should always be some barrier to prevent people from lazily taking the car again.

119355.explanation: We already provide financial support for Puuur Midden Delfland; this initiative is meant as a catalyst for tourism etc., but it cannot replace entrepreneurs joining forces.

119356.explanation: More tiny houses can be an acceptable temporary solution. The disadvantage is that such a solution often becomes permanent. Also, residents do not build equity in their homes.

119357.explanation: The battery technology for this is not yet advanced enough. It costs a lot, and you still do not have the equipment/fleet you would want.

119358.explanation: Dilapidation and decay are certainly undesirable, but legally there are few options to enforce adequate property use or to counteract use of a property purely as a long-term real estate investment. But we are open to feasible ideas.

119359.explanation: This prevents waste of advertising materials and thereby saves manpower and resources.

statements.title: There should be more tiny houses in Midden-Delfland.

titleParts.glossary: Small, detached houses of up to 50 square metres.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Midden-Delfland should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: See distribution center.

statements.title: Electric garbage trucks should be deployed as soon as possible.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in promoting tourism in the rural areas of Midden-Delfland.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in de bevordering van het toerisme in de

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
At least 30% of the new homes to be built in Midden-Delfland should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Midden-Delfland moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: Midden-Delfland should have a women's shelter.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: Midden-Delfland should renovate De Dorpshoeve.
A "yes-yes" sticker should be introduced for unaddressed advertising mail.

titleParts.glossary: Sticker on the mailbox indicating that you want to receive advertising leaflets and door-to-door newspapers.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Midden-Delfland.

titleParts.text: In Midden-Delfland moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: Residents of Midden-Delfland should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Midden-Delfland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should be able to require owners of long-term vacant properties to find a user or occupant.
